overview: Open world action RPG following Kliff and the Greymanes mercenary band across the continent of Pywel. Features deep melee combat with parrying, grappling, mounted combat, and multiple playable characters. Known for massive boss fights including mountable colossal creatures, aerial dragon chases, and a blend of swordplay with wrestling moves.
coreMechanics: Parry and Guard Break system — parry depletes boss Guard bar, opening damage windows
Grappling and wrestling moves — lariat, body slam, dropkick, throws
Weapon swapping between one-handed, two-handed, and ranged
Elemental imbuing — lightning, frost, storm, flame
Dragon mount for aerial combat and traversal
Mountable bosses — climb colossal enemies and attack weak points
Abyss Artifact progression — spend to unlock skills and boost stats
Meat-based healing — heal while attacking with quick moves
Multiple playable characters — Kliff, Damiane, Oongka with unique skill trees
Bell system — ring 8 hidden bells to reveal the full map

terminology.Kliff: Main protagonist, leader of the Greymanes
terminology.Greymanes: Kliff's mercenary band, the central group
terminology.Oongka: Playable Greymane member, berserker with rage abilities
terminology.Pywel: The continent where the game takes place
terminology.Abyss Artifact: Currency for unlocking skills and upgrading stats
terminology.Guard Bar: Boss defense meter — deplete it to stagger them
terminology.Parry: Block right before impact to interrupt attacks and drain Guard
terminology.Bells: 8 hidden bells that reveal map regions when rung

commonQuestions: How does the parry system work?
How do I break a boss's Guard bar?
How do I mount and climb bosses?
What are Abyss Artifacts and how do I spend them?
How do I switch between characters?
What's the best weapon type for this boss?
How do I heal during combat?
Where do I find unique weapons?
insiderTips: Parrying is the fastest way to break boss Guard — prioritize learning timing
Stock up on meat before boss fights — you WILL take damage
Heal while attacking with quick attacks, don't stop to heal at low HP
Some bosses can be mounted — look for climbing prompts on large creatures
Grappling moves deal high stagger damage and work on humanoid bosses
Ring bells early — they reveal the map and nearby points of interest
Unique weapons from treasure chests come with Abyss Gears pre-equipped
Ranged weapons are useful for aerial encounters and weak point targeting
Elemental imbuing adds extra damage — match element to boss weakness
Faction quests give bag rewards — check the faction menu for specifics

progressionSystem.primary: Abyss Artifacts spent on skill trees (Stamina, Health, Spirit stats)
progressionSystem.equipment: Weapons from shops, loot, chests. Unique weapons from quests/treasures
progressionSystem.skills: Per-character skill trees — combat, grappling, elemental, utility
progressionSystem.exploration: 8 bells reveal map, discover fast travel points and hidden areas

estimatedPlaytime: 30-50 hours for main story, 60+ for completionist
